merger_name: QIC – CopperString Project assets from Powerlink Queensland

acquirers.name: NORTH QUEENSLAND REGIONAL ENERGY CO PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
The Queensland Government has commissioned Queensland Investment Corporation (**QIC**) to oversee delivery of the CopperString Project, a proposed transmission line to be developed in North and Northwest Queensland, and to ensure the project is delivered efficiently while meeting the needs of those areas.

In order to facilitate QIC overseeing delivery of the CopperString Project, certain assets from Queensland Electricity Transmission Corporation Limited (trading as Powerlink Queensland) (**Powerlink**) will be transferred to:

* QBF No. 2 Pty Ltd (a QIC subsidiary) or another newly established QIC subsidiary acting in its capacity as trustee for the CopperString Project Assets Hold Trust (**SPV1**and **SPV1 Trust**, respectively); and
* North Queensland Regional Energy Co Pty Ltd (a QIC subsidiary) as trustee for the North Queensland Regional Energy Trust (**SPV2**and **SPV2 Trust**, respectively)

(together, the **Acquisition**).

The asset transfers will primarily occur pursuant to a regulation made under s 161 of the *Government Owned Corporations Act 1993* (QLD) (the **GOC Act**). Some assets will be transferred pursuant to an asset transfer deed. Both QIC and Powerlink are corporations owned by the Queensland Government. The assets being transferred include contracts, land and physical assets (such as office equipment).

SPV2 is ultimately intended to be a vehicle which will allow for private sector investment in the CopperString Project.

QIC is established under the GOC Act and *Queensland Investment Corporation Act 1991* (QLD) (the **QIC Act**). QIC is a leading global diversified alternative investment manager with over A$131 billion in assets under management (as at 30 June 2025).

Powerlink is also established under the GOC Act. Powerlink develops, operates and maintains the high voltage electricity transmission network in Queensland, supplying electricity transmission network services and related transmission infrastructure services. Powerlink’s network extends 1,700 kilometres from Cairns to the NSW border.

The CopperString Project is a major construction project to build transmission lines and substations in North and Northwest Queensland in two parts: the Eastern Link from Reid River (near Townsville) to Hughenden and the Western Link from Hughenden to Mount Isa.

The project is in its very early stages of planning and development, with the earliest works anticipated to commence in the second half of 2026. Major construction of the Eastern Link will begin in 2028, with commercial operations targeted for 2032 (subject to approvals). Construction of the second stage, the Western Link, remains subject to government determination.

Ultimately the CopperString transmission line is intended to connect the region to the national electricity market.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
In connection to the CopperString Project, subsidiaries of
Queensland Investment Corporation (QIC Limited) (QIC), QBF No. 2
Pty Ltd as trustee for the CopperString Project Assets Hold Trust
(SPV1 and SPV1 Trust respectively), and North Queensland Regional
Energy Co Pty Ltd as trustee for the North Queensland Regional
Energy Trust (SPV2 and SPV2 Trust respectively), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of the proposed transfer of certain
assets from Queensland Electricity Transmission Corporation
Limited (trading as Powerlink Queensland) (Powerlink), to the extent
it involves an acquisition of assets, any kind of property and/or legal
or equitable rights that are not property (the Acquisition).
The assets will be transferred to SPV1 (or another newly established
QIC subsidiary) acting in its capacity as trustee for SPV1 Trust, and
SPV2 as trustee for SPV2 Trust, pursuant to an asset transfer deed
and a regulation made under s 161 of the Government Owned
Corporations Act 1993 (QLD), as described in the documents
provided as part of the application. The assets being transferred
include contracts, land and physical assets (such as office
equipment).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirers will both be subsidiaries of the Queensland Investment
Corporation, QIC. QIC is established under the Government Owned
Corporations Act 1993 (QLD) and Queensland Investment Corporation
Act 1991 (QLD). QIC is a leading global diversified alternative
investment manager with over AUD 131 billion in assets under
management (as at 30 June 2025). QIC manages interests in Tilt
Renewables and Pacific Energy, which provide electricity generation
services. QIC does not have interests in any electricity transmission
services in Australia.
The vendor, Powerlink, is also established under the GOC Act.
Powerlink develops, operates and maintains the high voltage
electricity transmission network in Queensland, supplying electricity
transmission network services and related transmission
infrastructure services. Powerlink’s network extends 1,700
kilometres from Cairns to the NSW border.
Both QIC and Powerlink are Queensland Government owned
corporations.
The CopperString Project is a major construction project to build
transmission lines and substations in North and Northwest
Queensland in two parts: the Eastern Link from Reid River (near
Townsville) to Hughenden and the Western Link from Hughenden to
Mount Isa.
The Queensland Government has commissioned QIC to oversee
delivery of the CopperString Project. SPV2 is ultimately intended to
be a vehicle which will allow for private sector investment in the
CopperString Project.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is little or no horizontal overlap between QIC and the
CopperString Project in the supply of transmission services
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning vertical
effects resulting from exclusionary conduct post-acquisition
c. the CopperString Project will be a regulated asset subject to
various non-discrimination obligations
d. the Queensland Government will continue to hold the
beneficial interest in the CopperString Project assets.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC notes that it also considered whether the Acquisition fell
within the scope of sections 51ABU and 51ABV of the Act and the
potential application of section 51ABD (internal restructure and
reorganisations) of the Act. Having regard to the particular
circumstances, including the consequences of non-notification in
reliance on the exemption if it was found not to apply, the ACCC
considered it appropriate to make a determination on the basis that
the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
